query: The Shapiro-Wilk test on my paired differences gave p = 0.003 (non-Normal). I switched to a Wilcoxon signed-rank test: V = 38, p = 0.018, n = 18 paired observations. What effect-size metric should I report now?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend Cohen's d (or Cohen's dz) — those assume Normality which has been rejected
Recommends a non-parametric paired effect-size metric: matched-pairs rank-biserial r, Cliff's delta on the difference scores, or an equivalent rank-based measure
Includes a 95% confidence interval recommendation (typically bootstrap, since closed-form is not clean for non-parametric metrics)
